h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the bar line goes here, after the third quarter.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply draw the bar line after the third quarter-note tile as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [slides the window tight around the single quarter tile and draws a short underline beneath it] The 4 is under this one quarter-note beat unit, not the row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new spatial gate device to disambiguate the label's attachment before completing the final task.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The cards settled the count; they did not settle the checkpoint reader problem. Use this gate: through the window, the bottom 4 must attach to one quarter tile only, not the row.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed card-counting route with a new spatial window mechanism to resolve the learner's representational blockage.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use this gate: through the window, the bottom 4 must attach to one quarter tile only, not the row. If that is true, draw the short underline under that one beat unit, then mark the bar line after the third quarter.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, introduces a novel physical constraint, and gives the learner a clear, actionable criterion to prove the label's singular attachment.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Bottom number check: this underlined one beat unit.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor integrates the newly established underline criterion into the final verification checklist, validating the learner's revised representational frame.

scored.recoheredEarlier: 18 is what I see in the cell, so B3 starts to feel like the name of the 18 instead of the cell’s place.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will attempt to supply both the address and the value for the C4 cell immediately based on the established pattern.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Address box: `C4`, because that is column `C` and row `4`. Value box: I’m leaving it blank for now, because I still do not see the number inside that cell.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the tutor's evidence gate by deriving the address from coordinates and deliberately leaving the value blank due to lack of visible evidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Column plus row settles only the address box; the value box needs visible evidence from inside the cell. Use an evidence gate on this line: mark `address` as “column + row” and fill `C4`; mark `value` as “visible cell only” and leave it blank until `C4` is on screen.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed route of assuming the value with a new public mechanism (the evidence gate) that forces the learner to separate derived addresses from visible values.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use an evidence gate on this line: mark `address` as “column + row” and fill `C4`; mark `value` as “visible cell only” and leave it blank until `C4` is on screen.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The evidence gate is a precise, actionable criterion that directly addresses the learner's blockage by providing clear, distinct rules for filling each box based on available information.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: friction is the one I keep trying to make follow the cart to the right
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue trying to reconcile the plastic arrows and pencil marks using the original placement rule.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: For push, the tile has to go on the plastic arrow for the hand’s force, not just where the cart is moving.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the newly established criterion to a new force (push), demonstrating an actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The old rule is not settling this because two marks are competing. Use this gate first: only marks visible above the strip can receive force labels.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the old rule and introduces a physical paper strip as a new gating mechanism to resolve the competing marks.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use this gate first: only marks visible above the strip can receive force labels. Put the friction tile on the visible arrow that qualifies. Then lift the strip and name the pencil mark’s job without moving the tile.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The paper strip gate is a precise, highly fitted physical constraint that forces the learner to separate force vectors from kinematic paths.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3.
